rows.description: Soluta Varia
rows.notes: Top-centre page title clearly written and centered. No monetary figures on the title line.

rows.description: Allowed Rogers Pay for ye last year 1730
rows.notes: Amount columns well aligned with vertical rulings; rightmost three figures read as 0 | 13 | 4. 'Rogers' and 'ye' legible though handwriting slightly flourished.
rows.english_description: Payment allowed to Rogers for the last year, 1730.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment allowed to Rogers for the last year, 1730, amounting to £0 13s 4d.

rows.description: Pd Mr Shoemaker for a doz: & half of Knives and Forkes for the Fellows Table
rows.notes: Description legible; amount columns clearly show 2 | 4 | 0. 'Shoemaker' and 'Forkes' readable; no ambiguity in numbers.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr. Shoemaker for one and a half dozen knives and forks for the Fellows' Table.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr. Shoemaker for one and a half dozen knives and forks for the Fellows' Table, amounting to £2 4s 0d.

rows.description: Paid Mr Attterworth by Consent a gratuity for his great Care and Pains in detecting the Cooks boy anno 1723, who had melted down some of the College Plate
rows.notes: Long description is legible though some names (Attterworth) have uncertain spelling. Amounts align to 4 | 4 | 0; digits clear and within valid ranges.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr. Attterworth, a gratuity for his diligence in detecting the cook's boy who had melted down some college plate in 1723.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr. Attterworth, a gratuity for his diligence in detecting the cook's boy who had melted down some college plate in 1723, amounting to £4 4s 0d.

rows.description: Pd mr Brookes for bringg Lad Lane Kent
rows.notes: Description mostly clear; 'bringg' and place name 'Lad Lane Kent' slightly abbreviated. Amount reads 0 | 2 | 6 with good column alignment.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr. Brookes for bringing something related to Lad Lane, Kent.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr. Brookes for bringing something related to Lad Lane, Kent, amounting to £0 2s 6d.

rows.description: Pd Mr Reynell for comparing copies of certain Deeds with their Originals in relation to the Advowson of Mr Nymmet Devon
rows.notes: Amount columns read 1 | 1 | 0 but the surname and place ('Nymmet') are faint and could be spelled differently; chosen reading based on letter shapes and spacing.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr. Reynell for comparing copies of deeds with their originals regarding the advowson of Mr. Nymmet, Devon.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr. Reynell for comparing copies of deeds with their originals regarding the advowson of Mr. Nymmet, Devon, amounting to £1 1s 0d.

rows.description: Pd for a Bell Rope
rows.notes: Short description is clear. Amount columns unambiguous at 0 | 4 | 0.
rows.english_description: Payment for a bell rope.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for a bell rope, amounting to £0 4s 0d.

rows.description: Pd Mr Gaignier a year at Mise: 1730
rows.notes: Amount 2 | 0 | 0 clear. The name 'Gaignier' and word 'Mise' are legible but unusual spelling makes the reading somewhat uncertain.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr. Gaignier for one year, due at Michaelmas 1730.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr. Gaignier for one year, due at Michaelmas 1730, amounting to £2 0s 0d.

rows.description: Pd Mr Collison for Timber and Work on the Old Buildings
rows.notes: Very large amount clearly written as 40 | 0 | 0; name 'Collison' and description of 'Old Buildings' readable and aligned to columns.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr. Collison for timber and work on the Old Buildings.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr. Collison for timber and work on the Old Buildings, amounting to £40 0s 0d.

rows.description: Paid for Jewell at Christmas
rows.notes: Description 'Jewell' (possibly 'Jewell' or 'Jewel' spelling variation) is legible. Amount columns read 11 | 10 | 0; alignment clear.
rows.english_description: Payment for a jewel at Christmas.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for a jewel at Christmas, amounting to £11 10s 0d.

rows.description: Paid for the Accounts Dinner
rows.notes: Text clear; numeric columns show 0 | 13 | 6. Pence '6' is distinct and within valid range.
rows.english_description: Payment for the Accounts Dinner.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for the Accounts Dinner, amounting to £0 13s 6d.

rows.description: Paid for the Christmas Guardians
rows.notes: Amount reads 11 | 9 | 5 but the word 'Guardians' is slightly cramped; numbers are legible though pence '5' has a small flourish.
rows.english_description: Payment for the Christmas Guardians.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for the Christmas Guardians, amounting to £11 9s 5d.

rows.description: Pd Mr Combes for Pen, Ink & Paper for the Butler from July 3 1728 to Jan 8 1732
rows.notes: Date range and purpose clearly written. Amount columns read 1 | 6 | 6; zeros and sixes are distinct and align with ruling lines.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr. Combes for pen, ink, and paper for the Butler, covering the period from July 3, 1728, to January 8, 1732.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr. Combes for pen, ink, and paper for the Butler, covering the period from July 3, 1728, to January 8, 1732, amounting to £1 6s 6d.

rows.description: Allowed Mr Sadler for the Progress
rows.notes: Description legible. Amount 0 | 6 | 6 clear; spacing and strokes of digits unambiguous.
rows.english_description: Allowance to Mr. Sadler for the estate inspection tour.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Allowance to Mr. Sadler for the estate inspection tour, amounting to £0 6s 6d.

rows.description: Corn Prices
rows.notes: Short label 'Corn Prices' is clear; however it is ambiguous whether this is a header or an entry. Numeric columns read 0 | 2 | 6 but the line appears as a transaction in sequence.
rows.english_description: Payment related to corn prices.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment related to corn prices, amounting to £0 2s 6d.

rows.description: Pd for carrying off Rubbish from ye Old Buildings
rows.notes: Description legible; amount 4 | 15 | 6 is clear. 'ye' and 'Rubbish' handwriting consistent. All figures within valid ranges.
rows.english_description: Payment for carrying off rubbish from the Old Buildings.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for carrying off rubbish from the Old Buildings, amounting to £4 15s 6d.

rows.description: Pd for carrying off rubbish from the Kitchen yd
rows.notes: Second rubbish removal entry clearly reads 0 | 15 | 0; 'Kitchen yd' is abbreviated but legible. Columns well aligned.
rows.english_description: Payment for carrying off rubbish from the Kitchen yard.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for carrying off rubbish from the Kitchen yard, amounting to £0 15s 0d.

rows.description: Pd Mr Attwell 2 Guineas being a Fee to Serjeant Birch for his Opinion concerning the Title of Exeter Coll: to Hart Hall
rows.notes: Text clearly indicates '2 Guineas' which corresponds to 2 | 2 | 0; names 'Attwell' and 'Serjeant Birch' legible though with period spelling.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr. Attwell of two guineas, being a fee to Serjeant Birch for his legal opinion concerning Exeter College's title to Hart Hall.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr. Attwell of two guineas, being a fee to Serjeant Birch for his legal opinion concerning Exeter College's title to Hart Hall, amounting to £2 2s 0d.

rows.description: Pd Mr Wilmot for 16 Vols. Comm Prayer Books with 2 others L. Paps & One for ye Com: Table
rows.notes: Amount 16 | 5 | 0 clear. Some abbreviated words ('Vols.', 'Comm', 'L. Paps') are slightly unclear in meaning but readable; chosen literal transcription.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr. Wilmot for 16 volumes of Common Prayer Books, with two other large paper books and one for the Common Table.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr. Wilmot for 16 volumes of Common Prayer Books, with two other large paper books and one for the Common Table, amounting to £16 5s 0d.

rows.description: Pd Mr Baker for Dyer Lettinett
rows.notes: Amount 0 | 9 | 6 is readable. The final part of the description ('Dyer Lettinett') is uncertain in spelling; chosen as closest visual match.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr. Baker for Dyer Lettinett.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr. Baker for Dyer Lettinett, amounting to £0 9s 6d.

rows.description: Pd for looking after the Lamps from Oct: 20 to March
rows.notes: Dates and purpose clearly written. Numeric columns read 0 | 10 | 0 with clear alignment.
rows.english_description: Payment for looking after the lamps from October 20th to March.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for looking after the lamps from October 20th to March, amounting to £0 10s 0d.

rows.description: Pd Mr Collison for Work and Timber
rows.notes: Amount 21 | 0 | 0 is clearly written. 'Collison' matches earlier entries; handwriting for numbers unambiguous.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr. Collison for work and timber.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr. Collison for work and timber, amounting to £21 0s 0d.

rows.description: Pd for Candles for Mr Chap to 1 Day
rows.notes: Amount appears as 15 | 14 | 0; the high value is legible but the phrasing 'Mr Chap to 1 Day' is slightly uncertain in interpretation.
rows.english_description: Payment for candles for Mr. Chap up to one day.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for candles for Mr. Chap up to one day, amounting to £15 14s 0d.

rows.description: Pd for Oyle for the Lamps
rows.notes: Description 'Oyle for the Lamps' clear; numeric columns read 0 | 17 | 9. Pence '9' is distinct and within valid range.
rows.english_description: Payment for oil for the lamps.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for oil for the lamps, amounting to £0 17s 9d.

rows.description: Pd for Candles for the Buttry
rows.notes: Amount reads 9 | 17 | 9 but the pounds figure '9' is somewhat cramped and could be misread; context (series of candle/oil charges) supports this reading. 'Buttry' spelling as on page.
rows.english_description: Payment for candles for the buttery.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for candles for the buttery, amounting to £9 17s 9d.

rows.description: Pd for Candles for the Lodge
rows.notes: Amount 0 | 16 | 9 aligns well with ruling lines. 'Lodge' clear; digits are well formed.
rows.english_description: Payment for candles for the lodge.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for candles for the lodge, amounting to £0 16s 9d.

rows.description: Pd Mr Collison more
rows.notes: Final visible row shows 10 | 16 | 9 and a rule beneath the figures; description 'Pd Mr Collison more' legible. The underline suggests end of section but the row itself appears to be an additional payment rather than a calculated total.
rows.english_description: Further payment to Mr. Collison.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Further payment to Mr. Collison, amounting to £10 16s 9d.
